title: Health Command containing space no longer working with Podman 5.6
body:
### Issue Description

When using Quadlets that contain spaces in the `HealthCmd` the generated systemd unit cannot be activated.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Given this Quadlet:
```ini
[X-Container]
AutoUpdate=registry
ContainerName=authelia-redis
Environment=TZ=Etc/UTC
HealthCmd=redis-cli ping
HealthInterval=10s
HealthRetries=5
HealthStartPeriod=10s
HealthTimeout=10s
Image=docker.io/redis:8.2.1
Label=nix.home-manager.managed=true
Network=authelia
Notify=healthy
Volume=/home/niklas/stacks/authelia/redis:/data
```
The following `ExecStart` is generated:
```
ExecStart=/nix/store/j0m3hyxsmxgx2rca4km2q8gvr1xl2kgy-podman-5.6.1/bin/podman run --name authelia-redis --replace --rm --cgroups=split --network authelia --sdnotify=healthy -d -v /home/niklas/stacks/authelia/redis:/data --label io.containers.autoupdate=registry --env TZ=Etc/UTC --label nix.home-manager.managed=true --health-cmd "redis-cli\x20ping" --health-interval 10s --health-retries 5 --health-start-period 10s --health-timeout 10s docker.io/redis:8.2.1
```



### Describe the results you received

systemd activation will fail with:
```
/nix/store/5ay9a8afla7afjsqnkd5c68rpqyxz8fz-home-manager-generation/home-files/.config/systemd/user/podman-authelia-redis.service: 40:347 unknown character in \xHH form
```

### Describe the results you expected

I would expect the systemd unit to come up just fine. I also tried manually replacing the space in the `HealthCmd` with `\x20` myself, e.g.:
```
HealthCmd=redis-cli\x20ping
```
which then results in `--health-cmd "redis-cli\\x20ping" ` and the same systemd activation error.

Using Podman 5.4.1
```
HealthCmd=redis-cli ping
```
will result in `--health-cmd "redis-cli ping"` in the `ExecStart` which works fine.


### podman info output

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
This is actually a problem caused by sd-switch which got fixed already: https://github.com/nix-community/home-manager/issues/7560

Will close this.
@Tarow Not sure how the fix in sd-switch is relevant to this issue. I reproduced it as well.

The root cause is the change to `pkg/systemd/parser/split.go` made in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25722.

@Luap99 you commented that it might affect Quadlet and you were right. You also said that systemd should be able to deal with it, which is unfortunately can't. WDYT?
@Luap99 sorry, just reread the discussion and understood that systemd does handle it correctly, it's sd-switch causing the issue (@Tarow now I understand that as well). Sorry for the confusion

The error i got `unknown character in \xHH form` seemed to be caused by sd-switch being unable to parse the file during switch-plan creation, not by systemd itself.
After switching to sd-switch >=0.5.5, i can't reproduce the error anymore.

The `ExecStart` will still contain `--health-cmd "redis-cli\x20ping"`, but it is correctly handled.
E.g. when inspecting the container using `podman container inspect authelia-redis`, i can see the correct health cmd (with a space), which gets successfully executed.
